DECLARATION OF SARAH SHAPERO IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTENRATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (TRANSACTION ID # 78284150) FILED BY PLAINTIFF ZEIDAN, RASMI NASER ALZEIDAN, BAHJEH AZIZ
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-13-26 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION. BASED ON THE OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, THE PLAINTIFF ABANDONS MOST THEORIES OF LIABILITY EXPRESSLY ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT. FIRST, PLAINTIFF WITHDREW CAUSES OF ACTION FOR VIOLATIONS OF CC 2924 AND QUIET TITLE. THEREFORE, THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE. SECOND, PLAINTIFF DOES NOT DISPUTE THAT DEFENDANT WAS ENTITLED TO FORECLOSE ON BOTH ADDRESSES. (OPPOSITION 4:23-24). THEREFORE, ANY CLAIMS ARISING OUT THE PURPORTEDLY WRONGFUL FORECLOSURE ON BOTH ADDRESSES (AS OPPOSED TO JUST ONE), ARE DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE. HOWEVER, IN THE OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, FOR THE FIRST TIME IN THIS ACTION, PLAINTIFF CLAIMS THAT THE NOTICE OF TRUSTEE SALE REGARDING THE PROPERTIES AT ISSUE IN THIS CASE STATED AN INCORRECT AMOUNT OWED DUE TO INCLUSION OF DEFAULT INTEREST RATE. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE COMPLAINT IN PARAGRAPH 52 DOES REFERENCE FAILURE TO "IDENTIFY THE AMOUNT DUE IN THE NOTICE OF TRUSTEE'S SALE" BUT IN CONJUNCTION WITH "EL MONTE PROPERTY AND ARCADIA PROPERTY," NOT THE PROPERTIES INVOLVED IN THIS ACTION. THEREFORE, THE COURT TREATS THIS MOTION, IN PART, AS A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS, AND GRANTS IT WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE ANY APPROPRIATE CAUSES OF ACTION (EXCEPT FOR THE CAUSES OF ACTION DISMISSED ABOVE) ARISING OUT OF ALLEGEDLY IMPROPERLY CHARGED INTEREST RATE IN GOOD FAITH AND IN LIGHT OF HONCHARIW V. FJM PRIVATE MORTGAGE FUND (2022) 83 CAL.APP. 5TH 893. PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE 30 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER TO FILE AND SERVE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B).JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: K. VALDES; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication Of Issues

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-26-2026 Continued To Dec-14-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Of Issues, By Defendant Metropolitan Bank
Off Calendar Feb-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Sarah Shapero, Esq., representing Plaintiff Rasmi Naser Zeidan via Zoom Video Webinar.
William Murphy, Esq., representing Defendant Metropolitan Bank via Zoom Video Webinar.

Hearing held.

Defendant's counsel provides proof as to argument to the tentative ruling.
Defendant takes that trial date is set for late April and is requesting to vacate the trial date.
Plaintiff does not contest the tentative ruling and stipulates to Defendant's request to vacate trial.

The Court Denies the request to vacate trial. The trial is set in Department 206.

After hearing the arguments, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION.

Based on the opposition to the Motion for Summary Judgment, the Plaintiff abandons most theories of liability expressly alleged in the Complaint.
First, Plaintiff withdrew causes of action for violations of CC 2924 and quiet title. Therefore, those causes of action are dismissed with prejudice.
Second, Plaintiff does not dispute that Defendant was entitled to foreclose on both addresses. (Opposition 4:23-24). Therefore, any claims arising out the purportedly wrongful foreclosure on both addresses (as opposed to just one), are dismissed with prejudice.
However, in the opposition to the Motion for Summary Judgment, for the first time in this action, Plaintiff claims that the Notice of Trustee Sale regarding the properties at issue in this case stated an incorrect amount owed due to inclusion of default interest rate. The Court notes that the Complaint in paragraph 52 does reference failure to "identify the amount due in the Notice of Trustee's Sale" but in conjunction with "El Monte Property and Arcadia Property," not the properties involved in this action.
Therefore, the Court treats this motion, in part, as a Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings, and GRANTS it with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege any appropriate causes of action (except for the causes of action dismissed above) arising out of allegedly improperly charged interest rate in good faith and in light of Honchariw v. FJM Private Mortgage Fund (2022) 83 Cal.App. 5th 893.
Plaintiff shall have 30 days from notice of entry of order to file and serve an amended complaint.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)
